name: Marukai Wholesale Mart
tagline: Hawaii's first warehouse-style Japanese market — fresh bentos, sushi counters, and Japanese snacks you can't find anywhere else.
talkStory: Originally opened in the 1980s as a membership-only Japanese discount warehouse, Marukai was the first store of its kind in Hawaii and served the islands' large Japanese-American community. The daily bento selection is legendary -- arrive before noon for the widest variety of freshly made boxes that rival restaurant quality at half the price. The poke counter and sushi bar compete with dedicated fish shops. Membership is no longer required for food purchases, making it accessible to everyone.
